tone: Terse and technically confident — written for builders who hate fluff and recognize signal immediately. Every sentence earns its place.
do_list: Lead with concrete capability, not abstract value ('Agents wake from a forwarded email' not 'powerful automation')
Use short declarative sentences that assume technical literacy
Quantify setup friction honestly ('5 minutes', '$2,500 flat') — specificity builds trust
dont_list: Never use 'revolutionary', 'game-changing', or AI hype vocabulary
Don't explain what agents or memory are — the audience already knows
Avoid soft CTAs like 'learn more' or 'explore' — use direct action framing
